title: EPF Withdrawal — Complete Guide 2026
meta_description: EPF withdrawal 2026 — how to withdraw PF money online, advance withdrawal rules for home/medical/marriage, Form 19/31/10C, and UAN activation guide.

hero.one_liner: How to withdraw your Employees' Provident Fund money — online process, advance withdrawal for specific needs, and rules for full settlement after leaving a job

sections.heading: Overview
content: Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) is the mandatory retirement savings that both you and your employer contribute to during employment — 12% of your basic salary from you + 12% from your employer. The money accumulates in your EPF account earning 8.25% interest (FY 2025-26 rate) and is meant for retirement.
However, you can withdraw it partially or fully under specific conditions — from emergency medical treatment to house purchase.

Full withdrawal (Form 19 + Form 10C): You can withdraw the entire EPF balance after leaving your job and remaining unemployed for 2 consecutive months. If you've completed 5 or more years of continuous service (across employers if EPF was transferred), the withdrawal is completely tax-free.
If less than 5 years, TDS of 10% is deducted (no TDS if amount is below ₹50,000). This is the ideal scenario — wait the 2 months and get it all tax-free.

Advance/Partial withdrawal (Form 31): While still employed, you can withdraw a portion of your EPF for specific purposes: purchase/construction of house, home loan repayment, medical treatment (hospitalization 1+ month), marriage (self/children/siblings), education of children, or within 1 year before retirement. Each purpose has specific conditions and limits on how much you can withdraw.
You don't need to be unemployed for this — it's while you're still working.
The entire process is now online through the EPFO Member Portal (unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in). No need to visit the PF office or wait in queues.
Your UAN (Universal Account Number) must be activated and linked to your Aadhaar, PAN, and bank account. Processing typically takes 10-20 days for the amount to reach your bank account via NEFT/RTGS.

sections.heading: How to Withdraw EPF Online — Complete Step-by-Step Process

steps.title: Activate your UAN if not already done
steps.description: Visit unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in → 'Activate UAN' → Enter UAN, Aadhaar/PAN, full name, DOB, mobile → Verify with OTP → Set password. Your UAN is printed on your payslip or you can ask HR. If you don't have UAN, contact your HR department. First-time registration takes 10 minutes.

steps.title: Ensure KYC is verified at your end
steps.description: Login to EPFO portal → 'Manage' → 'KYC' → Verify that Aadhaar, PAN, and Bank Account are linked and status shows 'Verified'. If KYC shows 'Pending', your employer HR must approve it (2-5 days). Aadhaar MUST be linked to receive money to your bank account. Check this carefully before submitting the claim.

steps.title: Submit online claim (Form 31, 19, or 10C)
steps.description: Login to portal → 'Online Services' → 'Claim' → Select form: Form 31 for advance withdrawal (while employed, with specific purpose), Form 19 for full withdrawal (after leaving job, after 2 months unemployment), Form 10C for withdrawal of EPS balance. → Enter amount → Select purpose (if Form 31) → Verify bank details → Submit. The form is auto-filled with your data.

steps.title: Authenticate with Aadhaar OTP
steps.description: After submitting, you'll receive an OTP on your Aadhaar-linked mobile. Enter the OTP to authenticate the claim. Without OTP authentication, the claim is NOT submitted. Save the acknowledgment number for tracking.

steps.title: Track claim status and receive money
steps.description: Check status at: Login → 'Track Claim Status' → Enter acknowledgment number. Status shows: 'Pending' (still processing), 'Approved' (ready to disburse), 'Rejected' (check reason), or 'Disbursed' (money sent). Money is transferred directly to your Aadhaar-linked bank account via NEFT/RTGS within 2-3 working days of approval. Normal total processing: 10-20 days from submission to receipt.

sections.heading: Tax Rules on EPF Withdrawal — Critical Information
content:
5+ years of continuous service

Withdrawal is completely tax-free — no TDS deducted, no ITR required. This is the BEST scenario.
Service years across multiple employers count if you transferred your EPF (Form 13) when changing jobs. Check your EPF passbook for 'Date of Joining' — this determines your total service.

Less than 5 years of service

TDS at 10% is deducted if withdrawal exceeds ₹50,000 and PAN is linked. If PAN is not linked, TDS is 20%.
Example: You have 3 years service and withdraw ₹5 lakh. TDS = ₹50,000 deducted, you get ₹4,50,000.
You can claim refund when filing ITR if your total income is below taxable limit. File ITR to claim the refund immediately.

Transfer instead of withdrawal (best option when changing jobs)

When leaving one job and joining another, ALWAYS transfer EPF instead of withdrawing. Why? (1) Transfer is completely tax-free regardless of years of service, (2) Your service continuity is maintained (the 5-year clock doesn't reset), (3) Your money keeps earning 8.25% interest.
The process: Get Form 13 from old employer → Provide new employer's UAN → Submit to EPFO portal. Transfer takes 15-30 days.

Interest on inactive EPF

If you leave a job and don't withdraw or transfer your EPF for 3+ years, the account becomes 'inoperative'. An inoperative EPF account STILL EARNS 8.25% interest but you cannot take loans or partial withdrawals until it's reactivated.
Always transfer to new employer's EPF or withdraw within 3 years of leaving to keep your account active.

rows.label: House Purchase/Construction
rows.value: Max 50% of balance or ₹10 lakh, whichever is lower. Minimum 7 years service required. Submit property documents, agreement, or construction proof.

rows.label: Home Loan Repayment
rows.value: Max equal to full outstanding loan balance. Minimum 7 years service. Submit home loan statement showing outstanding amount.

rows.label: Medical Treatment
rows.value: Covered for self/family member hospitalization for 1+ month. Max ₹1,00,000 or actual cost, whichever is lower. Submit hospital admission letter, discharge summary, bills.

rows.label: Marriage
rows.value: Available for self, children, or siblings. Max ₹50,000. Minimum 7 years service. Submit marriage invitation, ceremony photos, or certificate.

rows.label: Children's Education
rows.value: Max ₹1,00,000 for higher education (graduation, post-graduation). Submit admission letter, fee receipt from educational institution.

rows.label: Before Retirement
rows.value: Within 1 year before retirement (55 years old), can withdraw up to 50% balance. Submit age proof or retirement letter from employer.

sections.heading: Common EPF Withdrawal Problems and Solutions
content:
Problem: 'UAN not activated' error

Solution: Contact your current or last employer's HR to get your UAN activated. If employer is unresponsive, visit the regional EPFO office with original payslips and Aadhaar for manual UAN activation.
Alternative: Call EPFO helpline 1800-1800-100 (toll-free) for UAN details.

Problem: 'KYC not verified by employer'

Your employer must digitally approve your KYC through their EPFO login (usually takes 1-5 days). If they're slow: (1) Follow up with HR, (2) Visit EPFO office with original Aadhaar, PAN, and bank passbook for offline KYC, (3) If employer has shut down, get KYC done at the EPFO office directly.

Problem: 'Claim rejected — name mismatch'

Your name must match EXACTLY across Aadhaar, PAN, bank account, and EPF records. Even minor differences (middle name, spelling variations) cause rejection.
Solution: Get corrections done at the source (update Aadhaar at UIDAI portal or bank), then wait 5-7 days for updates to reflect in EPFO system, then resubmit the claim.

Problem: 'Previous employer PF not showing in UAN'

If you have multiple Member IDs from different employers, they need to be linked to one UAN for consolidation. Login → 'One Member One EPF Account' → submit linking request with previous employer PAN.
Or visit the EPFO office with employment letters from all employers. Once linked, all balances appear in one UAN.

Problem: 'Claim stuck for more than 30 days'

File a grievance at epfigms.gov.in (EPFO's grievance redressal system). EPFO is mandated to respond within 30 days.
If still unresolved, escalate to the PF Regional Commissioner's office. You can also tweet to @EPFO or call the helpline.

steps.title: Login to EPFO Member Portal
steps.description: unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in → Login with UAN + password. If first time, click 'Activate UAN' and register.

steps.title: Check KYC status and update if needed
steps.description: Go to 'Manage' → 'KYC' → Ensure Aadhaar, PAN, and Bank Account are linked and verified. If any shows 'Pending', ask your employer to approve or complete at EPFO office.

steps.title: Navigate to Online Services → Claim
steps.description: Select the form: Form 31 (advance withdrawal while employed), Form 19 (full settlement after leaving), Form 10C (EPS withdrawal). Follow the form-specific instructions.

steps.title: Fill claim details and authenticate
steps.description: Enter amount, purpose (if applicable), select bank account. Verify all details are correct. Authenticate with Aadhaar OTP.

steps.title: Track and wait for money
steps.description: Use the acknowledgment number to track status. Money arrives in 10-20 days to your bank account.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw EPF while still employed?
faqs.answer: Yes — advance withdrawal (Form 31) is allowed while employed, but only for specific purposes: house purchase/construction, home loan repayment, medical treatment (hospitalization 1+ month), marriage, education, or if you're 54+ (within 1 year of retirement). You cannot withdraw the full amount while employed. Advance withdrawal limits vary by purpose (house: max 50% balance up to ₹10L, medical: max ₹1L, marriage: max ₹50K).

faqs.question: How long does EPF withdrawal take?
faqs.answer: Online claims with verified KYC: 10-20 working days total (5-10 days for EPFO processing + 2-3 days for bank transfer). If submitted offline through employer: 20-40 days. Delays usually happen due to: unverified KYC, name mismatches, employer not attesting, or high volume. File grievance at epfigms.gov.in if it exceeds 30 days.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw my employer's contribution?
faqs.answer: Yes. When you submit Form 19 for full withdrawal after leaving, you get BOTH your contribution AND employer's contribution (minus the amount diverted to EPS pension scheme). If you have 10+ years of service, part of the employer contribution goes to EPS (Employees' Pension Scheme) which gets converted to monthly pension — you cannot withdraw this part, only receive pension from age 60. For less than 10 years service, all employer contribution is paid as lump sum.

faqs.question: I left my job 5 years ago and never withdrew PF. Is it still there?
faqs.answer: Yes, the money is still in your EPF account. However, after 3 years of inactivity (no contributions), the account becomes 'inoperative' and stops earning interest. You can still withdraw it — login to EPFO portal or visit the regional office. For very old accounts (pre-UAN era from 2008 or earlier), you may need to visit EPFO office with original payslips and employer details for manual claim processing.

faqs.question: EPF vs VPF vs NPS — which should I prioritize?
faqs.answer: EPF (mandatory): 8.25% returns, auto-deducted, tax-free withdrawal after 5 years. VPF (Voluntary PF): You can voluntarily contribute up to ₹1.5L more per year (beyond the 12% EPF). Same 8.25% return and tax benefits. It's one of the best debt investments available — higher return than FD/PPF, with tax-free withdrawal. NPS (National Pension Scheme): 8-12% returns but only 60% is tax-free at withdrawal (40% goes to annuity). Strategy: Maximize VPF first (tax-free withdrawal), then contribute to NPS (better for retirement income security).

disclaimer: This content is for educational purposes only. Consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.
